commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a package-install `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` gate, updates manifest typings/docs, aligns prerelease ClawHub compatibility semantics, and adds installer/ClawHub regression tests.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +128, Docs +8. Total +171 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main is source-reproducible: the direct package install path enforces `openclaw.install.minHostVersion` but not `openclaw.compat.pluginApi`, while the linked issue supplies the older-host WhatsApp install scenario.
commands.review_followup: Remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the fail-closed compatibility behavior and normal draft PR handling; no automated repair is identified.

target.title: fix(plugins): reject incompatible package plugin API installs
target.body:
## Summary

- reject package installs when `package.json#openclaw.compat.pluginApi` requires a newer OpenClaw runtime than the current host
- keep the direct package install path aligned with ClawHub's plugin API compatibility check, including OpenClaw alpha/beta/rc CalVer host labels on the same API floor
- document `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` as the OpenClaw-owned install compatibility contract

Fixes #85869

## Motivation / Root Cause

`openclaw plugins install @openclaw/whatsapp` can resolve the latest npm package even on an older OpenClaw checkout. The install validator only enforced `openclaw.install.minHostVersion`, so a package whose `minHostVersion` floor still passed could be persisted even when its `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` required a newer runtime. The later plugin load then failed with an API mismatch such as `createSetupTranslator is not a function`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Direct package installs now fail before extension validation, dependency linking, or persistence when `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` does not satisfy the current OpenClaw compatibility host version.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout on macOS with Node v24.10.0, using temporary filesystem plugin packages and the real `installPluginFromDir` path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
npm view @openclaw/whatsapp version openclaw.compat peerDependencies --json
CI=true OPENCLAW_HOME=<temp-openclaw-home> OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION=2026.5.10-beta.1 node --import tsx src/index.ts plugins install npm:@openclaw/whatsapp
OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION=2026.5.10-beta.1 node --import tsx --input-type=module <temp WhatsApp-like package proof script>
OPENCLAW_COMPATIBILITY_HOST_VERSION=2026.5.27-beta.1 node --import tsx --input-type=module <temp WhatsApp-like package proof script>
```

Evidence after fix: Live registry metadata for the published affected package returned:

```json
{
  "version": "2026.5.26",
  "openclaw": {
    "compat": {
      "pluginApi": ">=2026.5.26"
    }
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "openclaw": ">=2026.5.26"
  }
}
```

The live registry install command on an isolated older host downloaded `@openclaw/whatsapp@2026.5.26`, rejected it before persistence, and left no `whatsapp` plugin directory:

```text
Installing @openclaw/whatsapp into <temp-openclaw-home>/.openclaw/npm...
Downloading @openclaw/whatsapp...
Extracting <temp>/openclaw-whatsapp-2026.5.26.tgz...
plugin "whatsapp" requires plugin API >=2026.5.26, but this OpenClaw runtime exposes 2026.5.10-beta.1. Upgrade OpenClaw or install a compatible plugin version and retry.
Also not a valid hook pack: Error: package.json missing openclaw.hooks

exit_code=1
plugin_dirs=0
```

For host `2026.5.10-beta.1` and temporary package metadata `openclaw.install.minHostVersion: >=2026.4.25`, `openclaw.compat.pluginApi: >=2026.5.27`, and optional `peerDependencies.openclaw: >=2026.5.27`, the real install path returned:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "code": "incompatible_plugin_api",
  "targetExists": false
}
```

For the same package on host `2026.5.27-beta.1`, the install returned:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "pluginId": "whatsapp",
  "targetExists": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: Older hosts now get an actionable compatibility error instead of persisting a package that can fail later during plugin load. The published WhatsApp package was not persisted on an older isolated host, and hosts on the same OpenClaw plugin API floor, including beta labels, continue to pass the direct install gate in fixture proof.

What was not tested: A clean same-floor live registry install was not claimed; same-floor acceptance is covered by the temporary package proof. The full test suite was not run.

## Regression tests

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.install.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/contracts/channel-catalog.contract.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/plugins/manifest.ts src/infra/clawhub.ts src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/plugins/manifest.md
node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/manifest.md
git diff --check
```

## User-visible behavior

Operators get a clear install-time compatibility error that suggests upgrading OpenClaw or installing a compatible plugin version.

## Compatibility and risks

This intentionally makes `openclaw.compat.pluginApi` the OpenClaw-owned direct package install compatibility gate. Optional `peerDependencies.openclaw` remains npm metadata and is not enforced as an OpenClaw install blocker.
target.author: rohitjavvadi

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
